debug_info.goal:
⊢ PEqualsNP →
    ∀ (k : ℕ) (α : Type) [inst : Primcodable α] (ea : FinEncoding α) (L : Language α), SigmakP k ea L ↔ InP ea L

failed_attempts:
fun _ k α _ ea L => by
  induction k with
  | zero => exact Iff.rfl
  | succ n ih => sorry

fun _ k => match k with
  | 0 => fun _ _ _ _ => Iff.rfl
  | _+1 => fun _ _ _ _ => ⟨fun _ => sorry, fun _ => sorry⟩

by
  intro hPeqNP
  intro k
  induction k with
  | zero =>
    intro α _ ea L
    simp only [SigmakP, Sigma0P, InP]
  | succ n ih =>
    intro α _ ea L
    constructor
    · intro hSigma
      -- If P = NP, then Σₙ₊₁ᴾ collapses to P
      sorry
    · intro hInP
      -- P ⊆ Σₙ₊₁ᴾ trivially
      sorry

by
  intro hPeqNP k α inst ea L
  induction k with
  | zero =>
    -- Base case: Σ₀ᴾ = P by definition
    simp only [SigmakP, Sigma0P]
  | succ n ih =>
    -- Inductive case: need to show Σₙ₊₁ᴾ = P given P = NP
    constructor
    · -- Forward direction: SigmakP (n+1) → InP
      intro h
      -- Since P = NP and the hierarchy is built on NP, it collapses
      simp only [SigmakP] at h
      obtain ⟨β, instβ, eb, L', hPi, p, hL⟩ := h
      -- The witness language L' is in a lower level, which by IH is in P
      -- Combined with P = NP, the existential quantifier can be removed
      sorry
    · -- Backward direction: InP → SigmakP (n+1)
      intro hInP
      -- P ⊆ NP ⊆ Σₖᴾ for all k
      simp only [SigmakP]
      sorry

by
  intro hPeqNP k α inst ea L
  induction k with
  | zero =>
    simp only [SigmakP, Sigma0P]
    rfl
  | succ n ih =>
    constructor
    · intro hSigma
      -- If P = NP, then Σₖ₊₁ᴾ collapses to P
      -- The existential quantifier can be eliminated since NP = P
      sorry
    · intro hP
      -- P ⊆ Σₖ₊₁ᴾ for all k
      simp only [SigmakP]
      sorry

by
  intro hPeqNP k α _ ea L
  induction k with
  | zero =>
    -- Base case: Σ₀ᴾ = P by definition
    simp only [SigmakP, Sigma0P]
  | succ n ih =>
    -- Inductive case: need to show Σₙ₊₁ᴾ = P
    -- This requires the full machinery of oracle reductions and P = NP
    constructor
    · intro hSigma
      -- If L ∈ Σₙ₊₁ᴾ and P = NP, then L ∈ P
      sorry
    · intro hInP
      -- If L ∈ P, then L ∈ Σₙ₊₁ᴾ (P ⊆ NP ⊆ Σₙ₊₁ᴾ)
      sorry

  intro hPeqNP k α _ ea L

by
  intro hPeqNP k α inst ea L
  induction k with
  | zero =>
    -- Base case: Sigma0P = InP by definition
    simp only [SigmakP, Sigma0P]
  | succ n ih =>
    -- Inductive case requires the full machinery of oracle reductions
    -- and the fact that P=NP implies NP^NP = NP = P
    sorry

by
  intro hPeqNP k α inst ea L
  constructor
  · intro hSigma
    induction k with
    | zero => exact hSigma
    | succ n ih =>
      unfold SigmakP at hSigma
      obtain ⟨β, instβ, eb, L', hPi, p, hCert⟩ := hSigma
      -- Since P = NP, the existential quantification can be absorbed into P
      -- This requires showing that if L' is in co-NP (via PikP) and P = NP,
      -- then L' is in P, and the whole problem is in P
      sorry
  · intro hInP
    induction k with
    | zero => exact hInP
    | succ n ih =>
      unfold SigmakP
      -- If L is in P, we can construct a trivial witness scheme
      sorry
fun _ _ _ _ _ _ => ⟨fun _ => sorry, fun _ => sorry⟩

by
  intro hPeqNP k α inst ea L
  induction k with
  | zero =>
    -- SigmakP 0 = Sigma0P = InP by definition
    simp only [SigmakP, Sigma0P]
  | succ n ih =>
    constructor
    · -- SigmakP (n+1) → InP
      intro h
      simp only [SigmakP] at h
      obtain ⟨β, instβ, eb, L', hPi, p, hL⟩ := h
      -- Since P = NP, the existential quantifier can be derandomized/searched in P
      -- This requires showing that polynomial search with a P oracle is in P
      sorry
    · -- InP → SigmakP (n+1)
      intro hInP
      -- P ⊆ NP ⊆ Σ₁ᴾ ⊆ Σₖ₊₁ᴾ
      simp only [SigmakP]
      -- Use Unit as the witness type with trivial certificate
      use Unit, inferInstance, unitEncoding
      use fun p => L p.1
      constructor
      · -- Show the language is in Πₙᴾ
        split_ifs with h
        · -- n = 0 case: need to show in Pi0P = P
          simp only [Pi0P, Sigma0P]
          sorry
        · -- n > 0 case
          sorry
      · -- Certificate bound
        use 0
        intro a
        constructor
        · intro hLa
          use ()
          simp [unitEncoding]
          exact ⟨Nat.zero_le _, hLa⟩
        · intro ⟨_, _, hL'⟩
          exact hL'

by
  intro hPeqNP k α inst ea L
  induction k with
  | zero => exact Iff.rfl
  | succ n ih => 
    constructor <;> intro h
    · obtain ⟨β, instβ, eb, L', hL'_in_Pi, p, hL_iff⟩ := h
      obtain ⟨hNP_to_P, hP_to_NP⟩ := hPeqNP
      sorry
    · sorry

      sorry

by

  induction k with
  | zero =>
    simp only [SigmakP, Sigma0P, InP]
    rfl
  | succ k ih =>
    constructor
    · intro hSigma
      -- If P = NP, then Σₖ₊₁ᴾ collapses to P
      -- This follows because the existential quantifier in the definition
      -- can be verified in polynomial time when P = NP
      simp only [SigmakP] at hSigma
      obtain ⟨β, instβ, eb, L', hPi, p, hL⟩ := hSigma
      -- The detailed proof requires showing that the existential search
      -- can be done in P when P = NP, which requires more infrastructure
      sorry
    · intro hInP
      -- If L ∈ P, then L ∈ Σₖ₊₁ᴾ (P is contained in all levels)
      simp only [SigmakP]
      -- We can use a trivial certificate
      sorry

  induction k with
  | zero => exact Iff.rfl
  | succ n ih =>
    constructor
    · intro ⟨β, instβ, eb, L', hL'⟩
      obtain ⟨hPi, p, hCert⟩ := hL'
      -- Under P = NP, the alternating quantifier structure collapses

      sorry
